prospects.thesis: Lockheed is the prime beneficiary of accelerating global defense budgets, with F-35 sustainment, missile defense (PAC-3, THAAD), and hypersonics providing multi-decade revenue visibility. The +13.2% 1M move on bullish options flow and institutional accumulation suggests smart money is positioning ahead of continued FY26 budget tailwinds and elevated foreign military sales driven by Ukraine/Israel/Taiwan demand. At 1.8x P/S with a $131B cap, valuation remains reasonable versus growth trajectory.

AeroVironment is the pure-play leader in tactical unmanned systems (Switchblade loitering munitions, Puma drones), directly benefiting from the Pentagon's Replicator Initiative and lessons from Ukraine drone warfare. The BlueHalo acquisition expands its counter-UAS and space capabilities, creating a differentiated small-cap defense platform. Bullish options and institutional posture confirm growing conviction in the autonomous warfare thesis.

Leidos offers exposure to defense IT, AI-enabled intelligence analytics, and cyber — the software-services layer of the defense stack rather than hardware. At just 0.8x P/S with expanding margins and record backlog, it's meaningfully cheaper than defense primes while riding the same secular tailwinds plus growing federal AI adoption budgets. Bullish institutional and options signals align with the recent 4.5% run.

Datadog is a direct beneficiary of AI workload proliferation — every new LLM deployment, agentic system, and GPU cluster requires observability, and Datadog's LLM Observability product is gaining traction with hyperscaler customers. The +11.7% 1M move on bullish flow suggests re-acceleration expectations into Q2 earnings. Rich at 23.9x P/S, but this is the highest-quality non-mega-cap AI infrastructure name available and provides watchlist diversification away from defense.

Kratos is a leading pure-play on unmanned combat aerial vehicles (Valkyrie XQ-58) and hypersonics targets, positioned squarely within the CCA (Collaborative Combat Aircraft) program awards cycle. Unlike AVAV's tactical drone focus, KTOS plays the higher-end jet-powered autonomous platforms and space/microwave electronics — providing a differentiated sub-niche. Recent -0.5% consolidation after a strong run offers a better technical setup, with bullish options and institutional signals still intact.

market_pulse.rotation_analysis: Here's what jumps out: big money is piling into Real Estate, Energy, and Healthcare — a classic "rates are coming down and Washington is friendlier than you think" trade. Real Estate loves the prospect of Fed cuts (cheaper mortgages, refinancing relief), Energy is getting a bid on the drill-baby-drill executive orders, permitting reform chatter, and continued Middle East risk premium, and Healthcare is quietly attracting flow because the tariff and drug pricing rhetoric has been softer than feared, plus M&A is picking back up. Meanwhile, the smart money is aggressively hedging Technology — that 19.3 put/call ratio is stunning and tells you institutions are worried mega-cap AI names are priced for perfection right into potential antitrust rulings, China chip export tightening, and the AI capex hangover conversation getting louder. Communication Services and Consumer Staples getting hedged points to worries about ad-spending softness and margin pressure from tariffs raising input costs on food and household goods, while bearish Financials flow lines up with widening credit spreads and LQD/HYG both flashing red — someone's pricing in loan losses or a credit event that isn't on CNBC yet. The contradiction worth noting: indexes are near highs but the options tape is defensively positioned under the hood, which historically precedes a rotation-driven choppy month rather than an outright crash — the S&P can stay flat while Tech bleeds and Energy/REITs quietly outperform. The non-obvious inference: with credit spreads widening while equities hold up, you should be watching the regional banks and private credit space — the bond market usually smells trouble first, and the fact that investment-grade credit (LQD) is getting hedged this hard alongside a bearish Financials tape suggests someone big is positioning for a polic
